title: alchemilla vulgaris var. asteroantha
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. asteroantha, commonly known as lady's mantle, is a perennial plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. Lady's mantle is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps, regulating menstrual cycles, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and creams, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
